intended_role: Senior accountant managing monthly close and audit support

organization.role_title: Senior accountant managing monthly close and audit support

contextual_signals.best_role_ever: Stable finance environment with clear close calendar and respect for controls.
contextual_signals.worst_role_ever: Chaotic growth setting where precision is treated as obstruction.
contextual_signals.current_energy_drain: Last-minute changes, vague ownership, and pressure to approve weak numbers.
contextual_signals.recurring_org_frustration: May slow movement when imperfect inputs require a pragmatic cutoff.
contextual_signals.relied_on_for: Protects accuracy, repeatability, and audit readiness.
contextual_signals.misunderstood_for: May slow movement when imperfect inputs require a pragmatic cutoff.
contextual_signals.unrealized_capacity: Strong accountant fit; risk rises in roles demanding constant ambiguous tradeoffs.

q2.text: I care most about stability, health, and being dependable to the people who count on me. In work I want to be trusted because the details hold up. A meaningful life would feel calm enough that I can do careful work and still have energy for family.

q14.text: During close a department submitted expenses late and the first version did not tie out. I stopped the process, documented the mismatch, and asked for support before posting. The timeline was uncomfortable, but the correction prevented a larger audit issue.

q17.text: People would say I get quieter and more exact when expectations rise. I check the source instead of guessing. Sometimes that can look like hesitation, but I am trying to prevent avoidable errors.

q20.text: I had to approve an accrual with incomplete backup near deadline. I used the best historical comparison, marked the assumption, and followed up the next day. I did not love it, but I knew the decision trail was visible.

q22.text: I would rate myself a 6.5 as a leader. I am steady and reliable, but I do not always push people hard enough when their missing detail affects the whole close.

q24.text: When momentum stalls I look for the missing fact or broken handoff. I get frustrated by vague requests and people treating controls as optional. I avoid confrontation longer than I should if I can solve the problem myself.

q25.text: I restate the detail and show the source. I try not to make it personal; I want the record to be clear.

q26.text: I naturally become the verifier and process keeper. Tension appears when others want speed but the numbers are not ready.

q27.text: I am building financial independence and a reputation for being precise. I value reliability, fairness, and clean accountability.

q28.text: Calendars, checklists, reconciliations, and documented controls organize my work. Future strain appears when growth adds transactions faster than the close process adapts.
